rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To File Records Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANTs TIMOTHY MARTEN and MARTEN CLINIC OF PLASTIC AND RECONSTRUCTIVE surgery's Motion To File Records Under Seal. Granted, for good cause shown, pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550(d). By previous order of the Court, Plaintiff has been permitted to file this case using a pseudonym. Defendant may file under seal exhibits to its motion for summary judgment and exhibits filed in connection with its reply brief to the extent that the exhibits are photographs of plaintiff or medical records that contain identifying information for the plaintiff, including her identity. No opposition was filed. The moving party shall prepare a proposed order that contains the specific findings required by Rule of Court 2.550(d), and in compliance with Rule of Court 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel in the top left of the first page of the proposed order. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD BAKER VS. ADULT ENTERTAINMENT BROADCAST NETWORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DATA CONVERSIONS INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts for constructive discharge. Furthermore, whether a reasonable person in the employee's shoes and faced with the alleged intolerable working conditions would have felt compelled to resign is a question of fact not properly decided on demurrer. Plaintiff's claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress are not barred by worker's compensation. Here, Plaintiff's intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress claims are premised upon Defendants' violation of a fundamental public policy, and such misconduct lies outside of the exclusive remedy provisions of the Labor Code. Leibert v. Transworld Systems, Inc. (1995), 32 Cal.App.4th 1693, 1706-1707; Accardi v. Superior Court (1993), 17 Cal.App.4th 341, 352-353. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
